judge.rationale: The candidate submission is written entirely in Dafny syntax, not Python. It uses curly-brace class bodies, Dafny method/constructor keywords, requires/ensures/modifies clauses, and a Main() method — none of which are valid Python. The entry point RetryGate is not a Python class at all, so the code cannot be imported or executed as Python. While the logical structure correctly models the retry state machine semantics (max_attempts validation via requires, failure counting, reset, remaining calculation), the implementation completely fails the task of producing a Python class. There are no Python constructs whatsoever: no __init__, no self, no def, no raise ValueError. This is architectural slop of the highest order relative to the Python task requirements.
